briefdescription.para: Sets the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance to its power-on default state.

detaileddescription.para: This function initializes the video stream parameters to default values, including resolution (1920x1080 @ 60Hz, progressive), color format (RGB), frame rate (60Hz), color depth, and pixels per clock based on the instance configuration. It also sets the hardware registers for frame width, height, stride, and memory video format. The function disables DONE and READY interrupts to configure the instance in polling mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance to initialize.

briefdescription.para: This function maps the memory video formats to the live/stream video formats.

parameterdescription.para: is the video format written to memory

simplesect.para: Live/Stream Video Format associated with that memory format

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufWr_Initialize - Initializes the frame buffer write instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function initializes an XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance, setting up its internal state and configuring the underlying hardware or driver instance. It clears the instance memory, initializes the lower-level frame buffer write driver, and sets the power-on default state if initialization is successful.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance to be initialized.
Device ID of the frame buffer write core (used in non-SDT builds).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful.
Error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Enables specific interrupts for the Frame Buffer Write core and globally enables interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: Also disables the auto-restart feature of the Frame Buffer Write core.

InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.
IrqMask
Bitmask specifying which interrupts to enable.
This function asserts that the provided instance pointer is not NULL, enables the specified interrupts, globally enables interrupts for the core, and clears the auto-restart bit to prevent automatic restarts.

briefdescription.para: Disable specific interrupts for the Frame Buffer Write core and enable auto-restart.

detaileddescription.para: This function disables the interrupts specified by the IrqMask for the Frame Buffer Write instance pointed to by InstancePtr. It also globally disables all interrupts for the core, and sets the auto-restart bit to enable automatic restarting of the core after completion.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.
is the interrupt mask the driver interrupt handler passes to the callback function.

simplesect.para: The function asserts that InstancePtr is not NULL.

briefdescription.para: Starts the Frame Buffer Write core.

detaileddescription.para: This function initiates the operation of the Frame Buffer Write (FrmbufWr) core for the given instance. It asserts that the provided instance pointer is not NULL, and then calls the lower-level start function for the hardware core.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance to be started.

briefdescription.para: Stops the Frame Buffer Write core.

detaileddescription.para: This function disables the autostart feature and initiates a flush operation on the Frame Buffer Write core. It waits for the flush operation to complete or until a timeout occurs. If the flush does not complete within the allowed time, the function returns a failure status.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the flush operation completes successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the flush operation does not complete within the timeout.

briefdescription.para: Waits for the Frame Buffer Write core to become idle.

detaileddescription.para: This function polls the status of the Frame Buffer Write hardware core until it becomes idle or a timeout occurs. It checks the idle status by calling XV_frmbufwr_IsIdle() in a loop, up to a maximum number of iterations defined by XVFRMBUFWR_IDLE_TIMEOUT.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the core becomes idle within the timeout period.
XST_FAILURE if the core does not become idle before the timeout.

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_FrmbufWr_l2 *InstancePtr, u32 StrideInBytes, XVidC_ColorFormat MemFmt, const XVidC_VideoStream *StrmIn)
memberdef.name: XVFrmbufWr_SetMemFormat

briefdescription.para: Configure the memory format and validate parameters for the frame buffer write instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets up the memory format for the frame buffer writer, validates the input parameters, and configures the hardware registers if all checks pass. It ensures that the video stream and memory format are compatible, the stride is properly aligned, and the selected memory format is supported by the hardware instance.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.
Stride (in bytes) for each line in memory.
Desired memory color format (XVidC_ColorFormat).
Pointer to the input video stream structure (XVidC_VideoStream).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the configuration is successful.
XVFRMBUFWR_ERR_FRAME_SIZE_INVALID if the frame size is invalid for the selected format.
XVFRMBUFWR_ERR_STRIDE_MISALIGNED if the stride is not properly aligned.
XVFRMBUFWR_ERR_VIDEO_FORMAT_MISMATCH if the stream and memory formats do not match.
XVFRMBUFWR_ERR_DISABLED_IN_HW if the selected memory format is not supported in hardware.
The function asserts that InstancePtr and StrmIn are not NULL.
The function copies the input stream structure to the instance.
For 4:2:2 and 4:2:0 formats, width and/or height must be even.
The stride must be aligned to 2 * PixPerClk * 4 bytes.
The memory format must be enabled in the hardware configuration.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves a pointer to the video stream associated with the given Frame Buffer Write instance.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the XVidC_VideoStream structure associated with the instance.

briefdescription.para: Sets the frame buffer address for the Frame Buffer Write core.

detaileddescription.para: This function validates and sets the memory address for the frame buffer. The address must be aligned to the AXI-MM width, which is calculated as 2 * PixelsPerClock * 4 bytes. If the address is not properly aligned, the function returns an error code indicating misalignment.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.
Physical address to be set as the frame buffer base address.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the address is valid and set successfully.
XVFRMBUFWR_ERR_MEM_ADDR_MISALIGNED if the address is not properly aligned.
XST_FAILURE for other failures.
The function asserts that InstancePtr is not NULL and Addr is not zero.
The alignment requirement is based on the AXI-MM interface and the core's PixelsPerClock configuration.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the current buffer address used by the frame buffer write instance.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr :
Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.
The address (UINTPTR) of the current frame buffer. This function asserts that the provided instance pointer is not NULL, then reads and returns the hardware register value corresponding to the frame buffer address.

briefdescription.para: Sets the chroma buffer address for the frame buffer write instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets the address of the chroma buffer for the given frame buffer write instance. It checks if the provided address is aligned to the required AXI memory width (2 * PixelsPerClock * 4 bytes). If the address is not properly aligned, the function returns an error code indicating misalignment. Otherwise, it updates the hardware register with the new chroma buffer address.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.
Address to be set for the chroma buffer.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the address is valid and set successfully.
XVFRMBUFWR_ERR_MEM_ADDR_MISALIGNED if the address is not properly aligned.
XST_FAILURE for other failures.

simplesect.para: The address must be aligned to (2 * PixelsPerClock * 4) bytes. The function asserts that InstancePtr is not NULL and Addr is not zero.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the address of the chroma buffer for the given Frame Buffer Write instance.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.
The address (UINTPTR) of the chroma buffer. This function asserts that the provided instance pointer is not NULL, then reads and returns the chroma buffer address from the hardware register.

briefdescription.para: Sets the vertical chroma buffer address for the frame buffer write instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets the address for the vertical chroma buffer in the frame buffer write hardware. The address must be aligned to a specific boundary determined by the number of pixels per clock and the data width. If the address is not properly aligned, the function returns an error code.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.
Address to be set for the vertical chroma buffer.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the address is valid and set successfully.
XVFRMBUFWR_ERR_MEM_ADDR_MISALIGNED if the address is not properly aligned.
XST_FAILURE for other failures.

simplesect.para: The address alignment is calculated as 2 * PixPerClk * 4 bytes. The function asserts that InstancePtr is not NULL and Addr is not zero.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the address of the chroma buffer (V component) for the given frame buffer write instance.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.

simplesect.para: The address (UINTPTR) of the V chroma buffer.
The function asserts that InstancePtr is not NULL.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the current Field ID from the Frame Buffer Write hardware.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr :
Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.
This function asserts that the provided instance pointer is not NULL and that the Frame Buffer Write configuration is set to interlaced mode. It then reads and returns the current Field ID from the hardware register.

simplesect.para: The current Field ID value read from the hardware.

briefdescription.para: XVFrmbufWr_DbgReportStatus - Prints the current status and configuration of the Frame Buffer Write core.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_FrmbufWr_l2 instance.
This function prints a detailed debug report of the Frame Buffer Write (FrmbufWr) core's configuration and status registers to the standard output using xil_printf. The report includes:
Pixels per clock
Color depth and AXI-MM data width
Enabled color formats (RGBX8, BGRX8, YUVX8, etc.)
Interlaced and tile format enable status
Control register value
Current width, height, stride, video format
Buffer addresses (luma, chroma, vchroma)
The function asserts that the provided instance pointer is not NULL.
This function is intended for debugging and diagnostic purposes.
